excerpt: The stock market gained 0.9% this week, with energy stocks rising and basic materials declining. Five9 and Bloom Energy led gains, while Roblox and Wingstop suffered significant losses. The S&P 500 is down 6.9% year to date.

content.introduction: The stock market gained 0.9% this week, with energy stocks rising and basic materials declining. Five9 and Bloom Energy led gains, while Roblox and Wingstop suffered significant losses. The S&P 500 is down 6.9% year to date.

# Weekly Market Update: Stocks Gain 0.9% as Energy Rises and Basic Materials Fall
Five9 and Bloom Energy were among the week’s best stocks, while Roblox and Wingstop posted big losses.
Five9 , Centene , Twilio , Nokia , and Bloom Energy  were the top performers among US-listed stocks covered by Morningstar analysts.
Stocks gained 0.9% as energy rose and basic materials fell; notable gainers included Five9 and Bloom Energy; notable losers were Roblox and Wingstop.

Chinese mainland equities were broadly stable over the week, consolidating gains following the prior week’s stronger-than-expected economic data and a limited pipeline of new domestic catalysts. The onshore CSI 300 Index, the main onshore benchmark, gained 0.9% (3.3% YTD), while the Shanghai Composite Index edged up 0.7% (2.9% YTD). Hong Kong's benchmark Hang Seng Index slipped -0.7% (1.7% YTD). The MSCI China Index, which primarily comprises offshore-listed stocks, lost -1.7% (-3.5% YTD).

content.outlook: Looking ahead, the figures. My colleague My Bui did some analysis on this over the past week. [...] --- [...] Over the past week, the US S&P500 gained 0.7%, even as technology stocks came under pressure following news that OpenAI was going to miss some financial targets. The news weighed on the broader tech sector. Market participants will continue to monitor developments closely for signals about the direction of sector performance trends and their broader implications for portfolio allocation and risk management strategies.
